stages.name: Raw materials
stages.shortDescription: Quartz, silicon metal, and the geologic inputs behind every wafer.

stages.name: Chemicals
stages.shortDescription: Polysilicon, photoresists, specialty gases, and ultra-pure reagents.

stages.name: Wafers
stages.shortDescription: Ingot pulls and polished silicon wafers.

stages.name: Equipment
stages.shortDescription: Lithography, etch, deposition, and metrology systems that build every chip.

stages.name: EDA & IP
stages.shortDescription: Design automation software and licensable IP cores.

stages.name: Design
stages.shortDescription: Fabless designers — Nvidia, AMD, Apple, Broadcom — specifying the silicon.

stages.name: Fabrication
stages.shortDescription: The foundries that print leading-edge logic at sub-5nm nodes.

stages.name: Memory
stages.shortDescription: HBM stacks and DRAM that feed AI accelerators.

stages.name: Packaging
stages.shortDescription: CoWoS, substrates, ABF — where chiplets become products.

stages.name: Networking
stages.shortDescription: Switches, NICs, and optical interconnects wiring AI clusters together.

stages.name: Assembly
stages.shortDescription: Server and rack integration by Foxconn, Quanta, Wistron, and peers.

stages.name: Datacenter
stages.shortDescription: Where the GPUs actually run — Ashburn, Dublin, Singapore.

stages.name: Power
stages.shortDescription: Nuclear, gas, and grid capacity behind gigawatt-scale AI campuses.

stages.name: Connectivity
stages.shortDescription: Submarine cables and fiber carrying traffic between regions.

megaLayers.name: Inputs
megaLayers.shortDescription: Geologic and chemical foundations that become production-grade silicon.

megaLayers.name: Toolchain
megaLayers.shortDescription: Machines, software, and IP that make advanced chip design and fabrication possible.

megaLayers.name: Silicon
megaLayers.shortDescription: Chip architecture, foundry capacity, memory, and advanced package integration.

megaLayers.name: Systems
megaLayers.shortDescription: Interconnect and server assembly layers that turn chips into deployable systems.

megaLayers.name: Deployment
megaLayers.shortDescription: Physical infrastructure that runs and connects AI compute at global scale.

nodes.name: Sibelco — Spruce Pine HPQ Mine

keyFacts.value: Momentive (quartz crucibles) · Shin-Etsu Quartz · Japan Super Quartz

nodes.name: The Quartz Corp — Spruce Pine Mine
nodes.tagline: Second-largest Spruce Pine HPQ producer; together with Sibelco supplies nearly all global semiconductor-grade crucible quartz; Hurricane Helene 2024 forced temporary shutdown highlighting systemic fragility.

keyFacts.label: Key customers
keyFacts.value: Quartz crucible makers globally · wafer manufacturers

capacity.notes: Smaller than Sibelco's Spruce Pine operation; precise capacity undisclosed. District-wide output ~200k MT/yr.
